welcome: Hey, welcome to Timbre. I'm going to ask you a few quick questions so I can learn your voice. Type or use dictation (Wispr Flow works great). No stress — you can always edit and refine your voice later.

audience.writing_for: operators, founders, and technical leaders taking their companies AI-native — pragmatic builders, post-hype, who care how AI actually gets adopted
audience.goal: leave them thinking 'I want this person building with us' — inspired and curious about the how, never sold to
audience.play_to: people who ship real things and can smell BS; lead with proof and the workflow, not the wow; allergic to AI-slop and buzzwords
audience.adapt_note: These are reference moves, not a voice to imitate. If the draft starts sounding like Alex, that's the clone trap — he's allergic to AI-slop and spots imitation instantly. Run the move in your own register. The persona always wins. Never feed another person's posts in as voice samples.
anatomy: Hook — a real moment or admission, or the build + an analogy. Front-loaded.
The pain / the turn — concrete, with a cost or a thing that didn't work.
The mechanism — in plain language, shown not claimed.
What it looks like in practice — scannable.
One spiky, ownable claim + the honest lesson, or the number.
A soft close (a real question), plus the proof/receipt.
playbook: Open on a real moment, not a thesis. Vulnerability beats the flex ("I was wrong" > "I crushed it").
Lead with a number / ship receipts. One real metric beats ten adjectives.
"How," not "wow" — explain the workflow; teach something saveable.
One spiky, ownable claim per post. Plain language, zero jargon.
Give the playbook away. Human and imperfect beats buffed and lifeless.
openers: "I reverse-engineered the X algorithm so you don't have to." (done-for-you value)
Bad: "AI can analyze your writing style and save you tons of time!"

questions.prompt: Great, {name}. Tell me a bit about your background — paste a resume or just give me the short version. Got an X or LinkedIn? Drop the handle (no need to log in anywhere).

Any writing you're proud of — a post, an essay, anything you've published? Paste one or two and I'll weight them heavily; your own writing is the strongest signal for your voice. Totally optional — just say 'skip' if you'd rather not.
questions.purpose: real writing samples, weighted above the easy answers (optional — skippable)

questions.prompt: Okay, the fun part — what'd you do last weekend?
Teach me how you'd make pasta — step by step, like a friend's in the kitchen with you.
questions.purpose: captures your words & tics + how you explain something
one smart follow-up to go a level deeper on what they just taught

questions.prompt: Two ways to say the same thing — pick the one that sounds more like you:

options.example: shipped the thing. worked on the first try, which means something's broken i haven't found yet. i give it until tuesday.
we shipped it — and yeah, i did a little lap around the kitchen. worked first try, which never happens, so i'm taking the win and worrying about tuesday on tuesday.

questions.prompt: Last one — which of these is more you?

options.example: cut onboarding from nine steps to four. shipped it friday. signups up 22%. turns out the fastest way to grow was to ask for less. do less. ship it.

we cut onboarding from nine steps down to four and shipped it friday, and by monday signups were up 22% — which taught me the fastest way to grow wasn't adding anything, it was having the nerve to ask for less.
